title: MANGA NGE The Eva manga in the United Kingdom
display_title: MANGA NGE The Eva manga in the United Kingdom
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] The Eva manga in the United Kingdom.             Quote Post by Nitecon » Tue Oct 11, 2005 8:10 am Hi there, Does anyone know where I can purchase the Eva Manga series in the UK? Is amazon.co.uk the best place? or are their alternatives? Also, which is the best set to get; "Old Format", "New Format", or "Special Edition"???

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 11, 2005 1:00 pm You're in London, aren't you? I'd try the basement of Gosh!, opposite the British Museum. Look for the 2nd edition - consistent size, unflipped, with all the special edition material, and the only format for vol9. Or amazon, and guess which volume they'll send you each time.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Oct 11, 2005 1:31 pm I tend to look around in Forbidden Planet and Travelling Man. They sometimes get random ones in, and they're pretty cheap. Alternatively, if you don't mind ordering vias the almighty Intertron, www.up1.co.uk are very cheap and tend to deliver any manga by the next working day. Last time I checked they had all of the Eva manga up to Vol. 8. About Â£4 each after p&p.

[]:             Quote Post by PilotIkari » Wed Sep 20, 2006 3:37 am My local Waterstones & Borders (Watford Branches) have tons of Manga...but never any Evangelion :(

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Sep 20, 2006 5:58 am PilotIkari wrote: My local Waterstones & Borders (Watford Branches) have tons of Manga...but never any Evangelion :(

[]:             Quote Post by PilotIkari » Wed Sep 20, 2006 2:03 pm Just spent the best part of a week waiting for my 'Next Day Delivery' of Angelic Days 1 & 2. Still waiting for my copy of the Eva-Prime vol.1 to arrive. Also of note, I did some work at the boarding school across the road from me, they've got students from all over the world, and I recently found a box load of abandoned Manga novels someone left there during the holidays. Unfortunately they were in Japanese and were Card Captor Sakura...

[]:             Quote Post by toReason » Mon Oct 02, 2006 2:33 am PilotIkari wrote: My local Waterstones & Borders (Watford Branches) have tons of Manga...but never any Evangelion :(

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 02, 2006 12:53 pm The Cambridge branch of Borders has a few copies of the first edition NGE manga, gathering dust.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Oct 03, 2006 1:13 pm the_seventh_child wrote: Anyway, amazon.co.uk probably is one of the best places! I have ordered a lot of Eva stuff from there, and i can say that it's really a good deal!

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Tue Oct 03, 2006 5:05 pm Mr.Tines wrote: Look for the 2nd edition - consistent size, unflipped, with all the special edition material

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Oct 03, 2006 5:12 pm Darius wrote: I knew there was a special collectors edition....but why 2nd edition and how do we know? But what's the difference between special and regular (details please)?

[]:             Quote Post by Darius » Tue Oct 03, 2006 5:23 pm Oh, I se..thanks. Well for me, I just buy all the ones that were published in 2004...since it's the most recent for the older books I believe

[]:             Quote Post by reagen » Tue Oct 03, 2006 8:50 pm Is it that hard to find in UK? And you got 3 formats? What are the difference?
